subject: Astronomy & Space
title: Vera Rubin and Dark Matter
description: A tiny lesson with a big payoff: Vera Rubin and Dark Matter.

hook.text: Vera Rubin found that galaxies spin wrong. The edges rotate as fast as the centers—impossible with visible matter alone.

buildup.text: In the 1970s, she measured rotation curves of spiral galaxies. Every single one was flat where it should have been falling.

discovery.text: The only explanation: invisible mass surrounds every galaxy. Rubin's work confirmed the existence of dark matter.

twist.text: Despite transforming cosmology, Rubin was never awarded the Nobel Prize. She died in 2016, and the award isn't given posthumously.

climax.text: The Vera C. Rubin Observatory in Chile, opening in 2025, honors her legacy. It will survey the entire sky every three nights.

punchline.text: She found the invisible 85%. No Nobel. An observatory instead.

quiz.question: What did Vera Rubin's galaxy rotation observations reveal?
options: Galaxies must contain invisible dark matter to explain their rotation speeds
Galaxies are slowing down and will eventually stop rotating
The Milky Way rotates faster than any other galaxy
